s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, check for runout of the steering rack.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the steering rack is placed horizontally.

s2.ptxt: If runout exceeds the maximum, replace the power steering link assembly.
Check the rack surface for wear and damage.
Secure the tie rod assembly LH in a vise.
Install the nut to the stud bolt.
Flip the ball joint back and forth 5 times or more.
Using a torque wrench, turn the nut continuously at a rate of 3 to 5 seconds per turn and take the torque reading on the 5th turn.
If turning torque is not within the specified range, replace the tie rod assembly LH.

atten4.ptxt: Perform the same procedure as for the LH side.

s2.ptxt: Temporarily install the 2 steering rack ends to the power steering rack.

atten3.ptxt: Do not fully turn the power steering rack without the steering rack ends as it may damage the oil seal in the rack housing.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, fully turn the power steering rack right and left 10 times to settle it.
Using SST, turn the control valve and measure the preload.
If the preload exceeds the specified value, proceed to INSTALL RACK GUIDE (See page ).
Remove the 2 steering rack ends from the power steering rack.
